User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the RentalSpot application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a property listing in the RentalSpot application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the property entity.

country.description: Country where the property is located.

city.description: City where the property is located.

bedrooms.description: Number of bedrooms in the property.

bathrooms.description: Number of bathrooms in the property.

phoneNumber.description: Contact phone number for the property.

whatsappNumber.description: WhatsApp number for contacting about the property.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores properties owned by a specific user. Path-based ownership to secure user-specific properties. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

definition.description: Stores all properties to display for the home page. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence to prevent needing a get() to determine the owner.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the property.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable solution for the RentalSpot application, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are Not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization of the `userId` in the `properties` collection.  This design allows rules to validate writes based solely on the data within the document being written, and the authenticated user's ID, without needing to perform costly and brittle `get()` operations to parent documents. This denormalization also makes batch operations more reliable. Segregation is achieved by storing user-specific properties under `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}`, ensuring that only the owner can manage these properties directly. The home page listing uses a separate collection `/properties` and denormalizes the owner's `userId` within each property document, to allow for querying all properties without needing to query by user. The DBAC principle is followed, as roles are derived solely from `request.auth.uid`.  QAPs are supported because `list` operations are secured by the path structure and denormalized `userId`, enabling efficient queries without exposing private data.
